message:
🌱 Add open-cluster-management: Natively support OCM on EKS mission (#962)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "open-cluster-management-514-natively-support-ocm-on-eks",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "open-cluster-management: Natively support OCM on EKS",
+    "description": "Natively support OCM on EKS. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current OCM installation on EKS hub",
+        "description": "Verify your OCM hub cluster installation on EKS:\n```bash\nclusteradm get hub-info\nkubectl get pods -n open-cluster-management\nkubectl get managedclusters\n```\nThis feature addresses the limitation that OCM originally only supported x509 certificate-based authentication, which is not natively supported by EKS."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Initialize the OCM hub on EKS with token-based authentication",
+        "description": "EKS does not support the default x509 certificate authentication used by OCM. Use token-based authentication or AWS IAM-based registration instead:\n```bash\n# Initialize the hub cluster on EKS\nclusteradm init --use-bootstrap-token\n\n# Verify the hub is running\nkubectl get pods -n open-cluster-management\nclusteradm get token\n```\nThe `--use-bootstrap-token` flag enables token-based registration which is compatible with EKS."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Join managed clusters to the EKS hub",
+        "description": "Register managed (spoke) clusters with the EKS hub using the bootstrap token:\n```bash\n# On the hub, get the join command\nclusteradm get token | grep clusteradm\n\n# On each managed cluster, run the join command\nclusteradm join --hub-token <token> --hub-apiserver <eks-api-url> --cluster-name <cluster-name>\n\n# On the hub, accept the managed cluster\nclusteradm accept --clusters <cluster-name>\n```\nFor cross-account AWS scenarios, the hub creates IAM roles for each spoke cluster. Non-EKS spokes can join by providing AWS credentials to the registration agent."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify managed clusters are registered",
+        "description": "Confirm that managed clusters have joined the EKS hub successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get managedclusters\nkubectl get managedclustersets\nclusteradm get hub-info\n```\nAll managed clusters should show `ManagedClusterConditionAvailable=True` status."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "OCM added native EKS support by introducing token-based and AWS IAM-based authentication for cluster registration, removing the x509 certificate requirement. Hub clusters on EKS use bootstrap tokens for managed cluster registration, with cross-account IAM role support for multi-account AWS deployments.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Initialize OCM hub on EKS with token-based auth\nclusteradm init --use-bootstrap-token\n\n# Join a managed cluster\nclusteradm join --hub-token <token> --hub-apiserver <eks-api-url> --cluster-name my-cluster"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "open-cluster-management",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "open-cluster-management"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "ManagedCluster",
+      "ManagedClusterSet"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/open-cluster-management-io/ocm/issues/514",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/open-cluster-management-io/ocm"
+    },
+    "reactions": 4,
+    "comments": 11,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "clusteradm"
+    ],
+    "description": "An EKS cluster for the hub and one or more Kubernetes clusters (EKS or other) to register as managed clusters. Requires the clusteradm CLI."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:40:19.142Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
